name: cimifugoside
mechanism: Proposed mechanism based on preclinical data: cimifugoside is a black cohosh triterpene glycoside that may contribute to anti-inflammatory and anti-proliferative activity seen in Actaea racemosa extracts. Human mechanism data are not established.
contraindications: theoretical overlap with hormonal and anti-inflammatory agents.
summary: Proposed mechanism based on preclinical data: cimifugoside is a black cohosh triterpene glycoside that may contribute to anti-inflammatory and anti-proliferative activity seen in Actaea racemosa extracts. Human mechanism data are not established.
